definition.summary: Spacing is a single-tier primitive scale - every value is an explicit multiple of 4px. There is no semantic spacing tier. Components reference hds.space.px* constants directly, which resolve to var(--primitive-space-*) CSS custom properties.

rules.rule: All spacing values must reference primitive.space.* tokens - no raw px, rem, %, or numeric values in component or page styles.
rules.invalid: gap: hds.space.px12  // tokenized, but the wrong value for this example

rules.rule: All spacing values must stay on the 4px grid. Off-grid values are not allowed.
rules.invalid: padding: hds.space.px12  // tokenized, but not the intended step for this example

rules.rule: Apply spacing tokens from the correct tier: sub-grid (px2-px6) for tight internal offsets, component (px8-px16) for padding, layout (px24-px48) for card and column gaps, section (px64+) for page rhythm.
rules.invalid: padding: hds.space.px64  // section tier used for a button

usage.condition: Icon padding, badge internals, hairline offsets
Button / input internal padding, tight layout gaps
Card padding, component gaps, column gutters
Page vertical rhythm, hero spacing, section separation

examples.name: COMPONENT PADDING - React inline style
LAYOUT GAP - React inline style
